SEARCH
Package de.uka.ilkd.key.gui.plugins.action_history

Class UndoHistoryButton

java.lang.Object
de.uka.ilkd.key.gui.plugins.action_history.UndoHistoryButton
public class UndoHistoryButton extends Object
A button consisting of an action component (normal button) and a selection menu (dropdown button). Selecting an entry from the dropdown executes an action based on that entry, pressing the normal button executes an action based on the top entry.

This class is a heavily simplified version of DropdownSelectionButton.

    final class 
    UndoHistoryButton.UndoAction
    The main purpose of this GUI component: the undo button / action.

  • Constructor Details

    • UndoHistoryButton

      public UndoHistoryButton(MainWindow mainWindow, int iconSize, IconFontProvider actionIcon, String prefix, Consumer<UserAction> pressedAction, Consumer<UserAction> pressedSelection, Supplier<List<UserAction>> actionSupplier)
      Create a new UndoHistoryButton with a given icon size used to display the selection component's icon.
      Parameters:
      mainWindow - the main window
      iconSize - the size of the selection component's icon (e.g. down-arrow)
      actionIcon - icon for the action button
      prefix - the prefix to prepend to every label
      pressedAction - callback if the button is pressed / dropdown entry is selected
      pressedSelection - callback if a dropdown entry is selected
      actionSupplier - callback that provides the list of actions to display

  • Method Details

    • setEnabled

      public void setEnabled(boolean b)
      Enables or disables the action button (enabling is only possible if the items aren't empty).
      Parameters:
      b - true iff the action button should be enabled

    • getSelectionButton

      protected JButton getSelectionButton()
      (Create and) return the selection button used by #getSelectionComponent().
      Returns:
      the selection button

    • getAction

      public MainWindowAction getAction()
      (Create and) return the main action performed in this component.
      Returns:
      the action

    • getMenu

      protected JPopupMenu getMenu()
      (Create and) return the dropdown popup menu that is opened by the selection component. Deletes all added components when the menu is null, thus leading a completely fresh and empty menu.
      Returns:
      the popup menu opened by the selection button

    • refreshSelectionItems

      public void refreshSelectionItems(Collection<JMenuItem> newMenuItems)
      Set new selection items/actions while keeping all other components of the popup menu.
      Parameters:
      newMenuItems - the new actions that can be selected

    • refreshState

      public void refreshState()
      Update the UI state of this action. If there are no actions to undo, the button is disabled.
